annotations\n+\n+import copy\n+import json\n+from pathlib import Path\n+from typing import Any\n+\n+\n+REPOSITORY_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n+POLICY_PATH = REPOSITORY_ROOT / \"config\" / \"eoat_location_normalization.json\"\n+_STORED_MACHINE_VALUES = {\"n/a\"}\n+\n+\n+def _text(value: Any) -> str:\n+    return \"\" if value is None else str(value).strip()\n+\n+\n+def load_policy() -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    \"\"\"Load and validate the single reviewed normalization policy.\"\"\"\n+    policy = json.loads(POLICY_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n+    required = {\n+        \"approved_by\", \"expected_location_state_counts\", \"machine_aliases\",\n+        \"owner_decisions\", \"physical_unit_splits\", \"supersedes_operational_migration_id\",\n+    }\n+    missing = sorted(required - set(policy))\n+    if missing:\n+        raise RuntimeError(f\"Location-normalization policy is incomplete: {', '.join(missing)}\")\n+    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n+    seen_rows: set[int] = set()\n+    for split in policy[\"physical_unit_splits\"]:\n+        units = split.get(\"units\") or []\n+        if len(units) < 2 or units[0].get(\"eoat_identifier\") != split.get(\"source_identifier\"):\n+            raise RuntimeError(f\"Invalid physical-unit split for {split.get('source_identifier')!r}\")\n+        for unit in units:\n+            identifier = _text(unit.get(\"eoat_identifier\"))\n+            if not identifier or identifier in seen_ids:\n+                raise RuntimeError(f\"Duplicate or blank normalized EOAT identifier: {identifier!r}\")\n+            seen_ids.add(identifier)\n+            for number in unit.get(\"source_rows\") or []:\n+                if int(number) in seen_rows:\n+                    raise RuntimeError(f\"Source row {number} is assigned to more than one physical unit\")\n+                seen_rows.add(int(number))\n+    return policy\n+\n+\n+def normalize_machine_reference(value: Any) -> str:\n+    \"\"\"Return an approved canonical machine number, or an empty value if unsafe.\"\"\"\n+    text = _text(value)\n+    aliases = {key.casefold(): str(target) for key, target in load_policy()[\"machine_aliases\"].items()}\n+    if text.casefold() in aliases:\n+        return aliases[text.casefold()]\n+    return text if text.isdigit() else \"\"\n+\n+\n+def is_stored_machine_reference(value: Any) -> bool:\n+    \"\"\"``N/A`` is owner-approved evidence of cabinet storage, not unknown.\"\"\"\n+    return _text(value).casefold() in _STORED_MACHINE_VALUES\n+\n+\n+def normalized_eoat_identifier(source_identifier: Any, source_row_number: int) -> str:\n+    \"\"\"Map a workbook row to its approved physical EOAT unit.\"\"\"\n+    source = _text(source_identifier)\n+    row = int(source_row_number)\n+    for split in load_policy()[\"physical_unit_splits\"]:\n+        if source != split[\"source_identifier\"]:\n+            continue\n+        for unit in split[\"units\"]:\n+            if row in {int(value) for value in unit[\"source_rows\"]}:\n+                return str(unit[\"eoat_identifier\"])\n+    return source\n+\n+\n+def normalized_source_rows(rows: dict[int, dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[int, dict[str, Any]]:\n+    \"\"\"Return source rows targeted at physical units without altering raw evidence.\"\"\"\n+    rewritten: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n+    for number, row in rows.items():\n+        updated = copy.deepcopy(row)\n+        source_identifier = _text(updated.get(\"EOAT Assembly ID\"))\n+        target_identifier = normalized_eoat_identifier(source_identifier, int(number))\n+        if source_identifier != target_identifier:\n+            updated[\"Original EOAT Assembly ID\"] = source_identifier\n+            updated[\"EOAT Assembly ID\"] = target_identifier\n+        rewritten[int(number)] = updated\n+    return rewritten\n+\n+\n+def split_units() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n+    return copy.deepcopy(load_policy()[\"physical_unit_splits\"])\n+\n+\n+def owner_approval_evidence() -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    policy = load_policy()\n+    return {\n+        \"approved_by\": policy[\"approved_by\"],\n+        \"owner_decisions\": policy[\"owner_decisions\"],\n+        \"policy_version\": policy[\"policy_version\"],\n+        \"supersedes_operational_migration_id\": policy[\"supersedes_operational_migration_id\"],\n+    }\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

{int(row[\"source_row_number\"]): _json(row[\"raw_values_json\"]) for row in imported}\n+        batch_ids = {int(row[\"import_batch_id\"]) for row in imported}\n+        if len(batch_ids) != 1:\n+            raise RuntimeError(\"EOAT Inventory source rows must belong to one import batch\")\n+        cursor.execute(\n+            \"SELECT source_file_name,source_file_checksum FROM import_batches WHERE id=%s\",\n+            (next(iter(batch_ids)),),\n+        )\n+        batch = cursor.fetchone()\n+    if not rows or not batch or not batch[\"source_file_checksum\"]:\n+        raise RuntimeError(\"Authoritative EOAT workbook evidence is unavailable from import provenance\")\n+    return rows, str(batch[\"source_file_checksum\"]), Path(str(batch[\"source_file_name\"])).name\n+\n+\n+def _columns(connection, table: str) -> list[str]:\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(\n+            \"SELECT column_name,extra FROM information_schema.columns \"\n+            \"WHERE table_schema=DATABASE() AND table_name=%s ORDER BY ordinal_position\",\n+            (table,),\n+        )\n+        return [row[\"COLUMN_NAME\"] for row in cursor.fetchall() if \"generated\" not in row[\"EXTRA\"].casefold()]\n+\n+\n+def _insert(connection, table: str, row: dict[str, Any]) -> int:\n+    columns = list(row)\n+    values = [row[column] for column in columns]\n+    quoted = \",\".join(f\"`{column}`\" for column in columns)\n+    placeholders = \",\".join([\"%s\"] * len(columns))\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(f\"INSERT INTO `{table}` ({quoted}) VALUES ({placeholders})\", values)\n+        return int(cursor.lastrowid)\n+\n+\n+def _append_provenance(existing: Any, message: str) -> str:\n+    text = \"\" if existing is None else str(existing).strip()\n+    if PROVENANCE_MARKER in text:\n+        return text\n+    return f\"{text}\\n\\n{message}\".strip()\n+\n+\n+def _eoat_id(connection, identifier: str) -> int:\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(\"SELECT id FROM eoats WHERE business_identifier=%s\", (identifier,))\n+        row = cursor.fetchone()\n+    if not row:\n+        raise RuntimeError(f\"EOAT identifier is missing: {identifier}\")\n+    return int(row[\"id\"])\n+\n+\n+def _clone_eoat(connection, source_identifier: str, target_identifier: str, source_rows: list[int]) -> int:\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(\"SELECT * FROM eoats WHERE business_identifier=%s\", (source_identifier,))\n+        source = cursor.fetchone()\n+        cursor.execute(\"SELECT id FROM eoats WHERE business_identifier=%s\", (target_identifier,))\n+        existing = cursor.fetchone()\n+    if not source:\n+        raise RuntimeError(f\"Physical-unit split source EOAT is missing: {source_identifier}\")\n+    provenance = (\n+        f\"{PROVENANCE_MARKER}: split from shared workbook identifier {source_identifier}; \"\n+        f\"this unit is supported by EOAT Inventory row(s) {', '.join(str(row) for row in source_rows)}. \"\n+        \"Compatibility relationships are shared evidence; audit, document, photo, and lifecycle history were not copied.\"\n+    )\n+    if existing:\n+        return int(existing[\"id\"])\n+    copied = {column: source[column] for column in _columns(connection, \"eoats\") if column != \"id\"}\n+    copied.update({\n+        \"business_identifier\": target_identifier,\n+        \"legacy_identifier\": source_identifier,\n+        \"display_name\": target_identifier,\n+        \"notes\": _append_provenance(source.get(\"notes\"), provenance),\n+        \"source_system\": NORMALIZATION_SOURCE,\n+        \"row_version\": 1,\n+    })\n+    return _insert(connection, \"eoats\", copied)\n+\n+\n+def _copy_shared_compatibility(connection, source_eoat_id: int, target_eoat_id: int) -> dict[str, int]:\n+    copied_counts: dict[str, int] = {}\n+    for table in (\"eoat_machine_compatibility\", \"eoat_tool_compatibility\"):\n+        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+            cursor.execute(f\"SELECT * FROM `{table}` WHERE eoat_id=%s ORDER BY id\", (source_eoat_id,))\n+            rows = cursor.fetchall()\n+            cursor.execute(f\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM `{table}` WHERE eoat_id=%s\", (target_eoat_id,))\n+            existing_count = int(cursor.fetchone()[\"count\"])\n+        if existing_count not in {0, len(rows)}:\n+            raise RuntimeError(f\"{table} has a partial prior copy for EOAT id {target_eoat_id}\")\n+        if existing_count == len(rows):\n+            copied_counts[table] = 0\n+            continue\n+        for row in rows:\n+            copied = {column: row[column] for column in _columns(connection, table) if column != \"id\"}\n+            copied[\"eoat_id\"] = target_eoat_id\n+            copied[\"source_system\"] = NORMALIZATION_SOURCE\n+            copied[\"row_version\"] = 1\n+            _insert(connection, table, copied)\n+        copied_counts[table] = len(rows)\n+    return copied_counts\n+\n+\n+def _reassign_audit_rows(connection, source_identifier: str, target_identifier: str, source_rows: list[int]) -> None:\n+    source_eoat_id = _eoat_id(connection, source_identifier)\n+    target_eoat_id = _eoat_id(connection, target_identifier)\n+    for source_row in source_rows:\n+        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+            cursor.execute(\n+                \"SELECT id,eoat_id FROM audit_records WHERE source_sheet=%s AND source_row_number=%s\",\n+                (WORKSHEET, int(source_row)),\n+            )\n+            audit = cursor.fetchone()\n+        if not audit:\n+            raise RuntimeError(f\"Missing audit record for EOAT Inventory row {source_row}\")\n+        if int(audit[\"eoat_id\"]) not in {source_eoat_id, target_eoat_id}:\n+            raise RuntimeError(f\"Audit row {source_row} is linked to an unexpected EOAT\")\n+        if int(audit[\"eoat_id\"]) != target_eoat_id:\n+            with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+                cursor.execute(\"UPDATE audit_records SET eoat_id=%s,row_version=row_version+1 WHERE id=%s\", (target_eoat_id, audit[\"id\"]))\n+\n+\n+def _apply_physical_unit_splits(connection, policy: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n+    report: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n+    for split in policy[\"physical_unit_splits\"]:\n+        source_identifier = str(split[\"source_identifier\"])\n+        source_eoat_id = _eoat_id(connection, source_identifier)\n+        unit_identifiers = [str(unit[\"eoat_identifier\"]) for unit in split[\"units\"]]\n+        provenance = (\n+            f\"{PROVENANCE_MARKER}: shared workbook identifier split into physical units \"\n+            f\"{', '.join(unit_identifiers)}. {split['reason']}\"\n+        )\n+        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+            cursor.execute(\n+                \"UPDATE eoats SET notes=%s,source_system=%s,row_version=row_version+1 WHERE id=%s \"\n+                \"AND notes NOT LIKE %s\",\n+                (_append_provenance(_notes(connection, source_eoat_id), provenance), NORMALIZATION_SOURCE, source_eoat_id, f\"%{PROVENANCE_MARKER}%\"),\n+            )\n+        copied: Counter[str] = Counter()\n+        for unit in split[\"units\"]:\n+            target_identifier = str(unit[\"eoat_identifier\"])\n+            target_eoat_id = _clone_eoat(connection, source_identifier, target_identifier, list(unit[\"source_rows\"]))\n+            if target_identifier != source_identifier:\n+                copied.update(_copy_shared_compatibility(connection, source_eoat_id, target_eoat_id))\n+            _reassign_audit_rows(connection, source_identifier, target_identifier, list(unit[\"source_rows\"]))\n+        report.append({\n+            \"source_identifier\": source_identifier,\n+            \"normalized_unit_identifiers\": unit_identifiers,\n+            \"reason\": split[\"reason\"],\n+            \"copied_shared_relationships\": dict(sorted(copied.items())),\n+        })\n+    return report\n+\n+\n+def _notes(connection, eoat_id: int) -> str:\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(\"SELECT notes FROM eoats WHERE id=%s\", (eoat_id,))\n+        row = cursor.fetchone()\n+    return \"\" if not row or row[\"notes\"] is None else str(row[\"notes\"])\n+\n+\n+def _apply_machine_aliases(connection, rows: dict[int, dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n+    applied = []\n+    for source_row, row in rows.items():\n+        canonical = normalize_machine_reference(row.get(\"Press/Machine #\"))\n+        raw = str(row.get(\"Press/Machine #\") or \"\").strip()\n+        if not canonical or raw == canonical:\n+            continue\n+        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+            cursor.execute(\n+                \"SELECT m.id FROM machines m JOIN plants p ON p.id=m.plant_id \"\n+                \"WHERE p.plant_code=%s AND m.machine_number=%s\",\n+                (\"CL\" if str(row.get(\"Plant/Area\") or \"\").casefold() == \"cleanroom\" else \"P4\", canonical),\n+            )\n+            machines = cursor.fetchall()\n+            if len(machines) != 1:\n+                raise RuntimeError(f\"Could not resolve exactly one canonical machine {canonical} for source row {source_row}\")\n+            machine_id = int(machines[0][\"id\"])\n+            cursor.execute(\n+                \"UPDATE import_rows SET normalized_values_json=JSON_SET(COALESCE(normalized_values_json,JSON_OBJECT()), '$.machine', %s) \"\n+                \"WHERE source_sheet=%s AND source_row_number=%s\",\n+                (canonical, WORKSHEET, source_row),\n+            )\n+            cursor.execute(\n+                \"UPDATE audit_records SET machine_id=%s,row_version=row_version+1 \"\n+                \"WHERE source_sheet=%s AND source_row_number=%s\",\n+                (machine_id, WORKSHEET, source_row),\n+            )\n+        applied.append({\"source_row_number\": source_row, \"original_machine\": raw, \"normalized_machine\": canonical})\n+    return applied\n+\n+\n+def _replace_location_evidence(connection, rows: dict[int, dict[str, Any]], digest: str, workbook_name: str, policy: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    plan = build_plan(\n+        normalized_source_rows(rows), load_database(connection),\n+        workbook_sha256=digest, source_workbook=workbook_name,\n+    )\n+    if plan[\"state_counts\"] != policy[\"expected_location_state_counts\"]:\n+        raise RuntimeError(\n+            f\"Owner-approved location totals do not match: {plan['state_counts']} != {policy['expected_location_state_counts']}\"\n+        )\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(\"DELETE FROM eoat_location_assertions\")\n+        cursor.execute(\"DELETE FROM eoat_location_observations\")\n+    apply_plan(connection, plan, commit=False)\n+    return plan\n+\n+\n+def correct(connection, *, apply: bool) -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    policy = load_policy()\n+    identity = database_identity(connection)\n+    assert_safe_source(identity)\n+    if identity[\"alembic_revision\"] != REQUIRED_REVISION:\n+        raise RuntimeError(f\"Schema mismatch: required {REQUIRED_REVISION}, found {identity['alembic_revision']}\")\n+    rows, digest, workbook_name = _source_rows(connection)\n+    if not apply:\n+        planned_rows = normalized_source_rows(rows)\n+        return {\n+            \"status\": \"PLANNED\",\n+            \"database\": identity[\"database_name\"],\n+            \"source_workbook\": workbook_name,\n+            \"source_workbook_sha256\": digest,\n+            \"approval\": owner_approval_evidence(),\n+            \"physical_unit_splits\": policy[\"physical_unit_splits\"],\n+            \"planned_source_row_targets\": {\n+                str(number): target[\"EOAT Assembly ID\"] for number, target in planned_rows.items()\n+                if target.get(\"Original EOAT Assembly ID\")\n+            },\n+        }\n+    try:\n+        aliases = _apply_machine_aliases(connection, rows)\n+        split_report = _apply_physical_unit_splits(connection, policy)\n+        plan = _replace_location_evidence(connection, rows, digest, workbook_name, policy)\n+        connection.commit()\n+    except Exception:\n+        connection.rollback()\n+        raise\n+    return {\n+        \"status\": \"APPLIED\",\n+        \"database\": identity[\"database_name\"],\n+        \"source_workbook\": workbook_name,\n+        \"source_workbook_sha256\": digest,\n+        \"approval\": owner_approval_evidence(),\n+        \"machine_aliases_applied\": aliases,\n+        \"physical_unit_splits\": split_report,\n+        \"location_observation_count\": len(plan[\"observations\"]),\n+        \"location_assertion_count\": len(plan[\"assertions\"]),\n+        \"location_state_counts\": plan[\"state_counts\"],\n+    }\n+\n+\n+def main() -> int:\n+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Apply owner-approved EOAT observed-location normalization to development\")\n+    parser.add_argument(\"--environment\", default=\"development\")\n+    parser.add_argument(\"--apply\", action=\"store_true\")\n+    parser.add_argument(\"--report\", type=Path, required=True)\n+    args = parser.parse_args()\n+    from scripts.database.production_data_migration import read_env\n+\n+    values = read_env(args.environment)\n+    with connect(values) as connection:\n+        report = correct(connection, apply=args.apply)\n+    args.report.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n+    write_json(args.report, report)\n+    print(json.dumps({\"status\": report[\"status\"], \"report\": str(args.report)}, indent=2))\n+    return 0\n+\n+\n+if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n+    raise SystemExit(main())\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

SUPERSEDED_OPERATIONAL_MIGRATION_ID:\n+        raise RuntimeError(\"Owner-approved location policy does not name the superseded migration\")\n+    if not policy.get(\"owner_decisions\") or not policy.get(\"approved_by\"):\n+        raise RuntimeError(\"Owner-approved location policy is incomplete\")\n+    return policy\n+\n+\n+def duplicate_resolution_report(connection, policy: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n+        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n+            SELECT e.business_identifier,a.source_row_number,m.machine_number\n+            FROM audit_records a JOIN eoats e ON e.id=a.eoat_id\n+            LEFT JOIN machines m ON m.id=a.machine_id\n+            WHERE a.source_sheet='EOAT Inventory' AND a.source_row_number IN (81,82,83,85,86,88,89,92,93)\n+            ORDER BY a.source_row_number\n+        \"\"\")\n+        split_audits = cursor.fetchall()\n+        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n+            SELECT e.business_identifier,a.source_row_number,a.state,a.machine_id,a.storage_location_id\n+            FROM eoat_location_assertions a JOIN eoats e ON e.id=a.eoat_id\n+            WHERE a.original_source_wording LIKE '%Press/Machine #=N/A%'\n+            ORDER BY a.source_row_number\n+        \"\"\")\n+        na_rows = cursor.fetchall()\n+        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n+            SELECT i.source_row_number,JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(i.raw_values_json,'$.\"Press/Machine #\"')) original_machine,\n+                   JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(i.normalized_values_json,'$.machine')) normalized_machine,m.machine_number AS audit_machine\n+            FROM import_rows i LEFT JOIN audit_records a ON a.source_sheet=i.source_sheet AND a.source_row_number=i.source_row_number\n+            LEFT JOIN machines m ON m.id=a.machine_id\n+            WHERE i.source_sheet='EOAT Inventory'\n+              AND JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(i.raw_values_json,'$.\"Press/Machine #\"'))='26 - Xqual in 25'\n+            ORDER BY i.source_row_number\n+        \"\"\")\n+        xqual_rows = cursor.fetchall()\n+        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n+            SELECT e.business_identifier,o.state,o.machine_id,o.storage_location_id,o.original_source_wording\n+            FROM eoat_location_observations o JOIN eoats e ON e.id=o.eoat_id\n+            WHERE o.state IN ('UNKNOWN','CONFLICTING') ORDER BY e.business_identifier\n+        \"\"\")\n+        unresolved = cursor.fetchall()\n+    if any(row[\"state\"] != \"STORED\" or row[\"machine_id\"] is not None or any(row[\"normalized_machine\"] != \"26\" or row[\"audit_machine\"] != \"26\" for row in xqual_rows):\n+        raise RuntimeError(\"Xqual source wording was not normalized to Machine 26\")\n+    if \"physical_unit_splits\": policy[\"physical_unit_splits\"],\n+        \"actual_split_audit_assignments\": split_audits,\n+        \"na_storage_normalizations\": na_rows,\n+        \"machine_26_normalizations\": xqual_rows,\n+        \"plant4_movement_resolutions\": policy.get(\"plant4_movement_resolutions\", []),\n+        \"remaining_unresolved_records\": unresolved,\n+    }\n*** End Patch";

const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport json\nfrom scripts.database.production_data_migration import read_env, connect\nwith connect(read_env('development')) as c:\n  with c.cursor() as x:\n    x.execute(\"\"\"SELECT e.business_identifier,o.state,m.machine_number,o.storage_location_id,o.source_row_number\n      FROM eoat_location_observations o JOIN eoats e ON e.id=o.eoat_id LEFT JOIN machines m ON m.id=o.machine_id\n      WHERE e.business_identifier IN ('P4-EOAT-0010','P4-EOAT-0027','P4-EOAT-0029','P4-EOAT-0030','P4-EOAT-0042','P4-EOAT-0057')\n      ORDER BY e.business_identifier\"\"\")\n    states=x.fetchall()\n    x.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) count FROM eoat_installations\")\n    installations=x.fetchone()['count']\n    x.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) count FROM eoat_storage_assignments\")\n    storage=x.fetchone()['count']\n    x.execute(\"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) count FROM documents d JOIN document_links l ON l.document_id=d.id\n      JOIN eoats e ON l.entity_type='eoat' AND l.entity_id=e.id WHERE e.business_identifier IN ('CL-EOAT-0057','CL-EOAT-0058','CL-EOAT-0059','CL-EOAT-0060','CL-EOAT-0061')\"\"\")\n    clone_docs=x.fetchone()['count']\n    x.execute(\"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) count FROM photos p JOIN eoats e ON p.eoat_id=e.id\n      WHERE e.business_identifier IN ('CL-EOAT-0057','CL-EOAT-0058','CL-EOAT-0059','CL-EOAT-0060','CL-EOAT-0061')\"\"\")\n    clone_photos=x.fetchone()['count']\n  print(json.dumps({'selected_current_states':states,'lifecycle_installations':installations,'lifecycle_storage_assignments':storage,'clone_document_links':clone_docs,'clone_photos':clone_photos},indent=2,default=str))\n'@ | python -","timeout_ms":30000,"workdir":"C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction"});

**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T18:56:38.863Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction\\docs\\database\\eoat_location_observations.md\n@@\n The five normalized states are `INSTALLED`, `STORED`, `UNKNOWN`, `INACTIVE`, and `CONFLICTING`. A stored observation may have no `storage_location_id`; this means storage is proven but the cabinet/location is unspecified. It must not be replaced by a made-up generic cabinet.\n+\n+## Owner-approved workbook normalization\n+\n+The reviewed policy in `config/eoat_location_normalization.json` is authoritative for the operational migration:\n+\n+- An audited `Press/Machine #` value of `N/A` means `STORED`, with both `machine_id` and `storage_location_id` null.\n+- `26 - Xqual in 25` is retained in raw workbook/import evidence but normalizes to Machine `26` everywhere a machine relationship is represented.\n+- Same-day Cleanroom evidence that proves simultaneous identical units is split into physical EOAT masters. The preserved original and the deterministic added IDs retain the shared compatibility relationships; only the evidence row assigned to a unit moves with that unit. Documents, photos, lifecycle history, and unrelated audits are not copied.\n+- Plant 4 multi-machine audit sequences without duplicate-pair proof are treated as movement evidence, not physical duplicates. When no present machine is reliable, the observed state is `STORED` with cabinet unspecified.\n+\n+These are observed-current-state decisions, not installation/removal/storage lifecycle events. They never invent event timestamps, cabinet identifiers, actors, or lifecycle history.\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction\\docs\\PRODUCTION_OPERATIONAL_DATA_MIGRATION.md\n@@\n-- The source must be the authoritative development MySQL database and must be at Alembic revision `20260715_0006`.\n+- The source must be the authoritative development MySQL database and must be at Alembic revision `20260717_0007`.\n@@\n The output contains the SQL artifact and checksum, manifest, all-table classification, source/expected counts, seed parity, exclusions and transformations, file-reference analysis, validation report, a standalone copy of the migration utility, and controlled import/rollback instructions.\n+\n+For the approved observed-location correction, the package also contains the owner approval evidence, required empty-production baseline counts, location observation/assertion report, duplicate-resolution report, and an explicit supersession record for `eoat-operational-633d0596386fc44b33c2`. The superseded package must never be amended or overwritten.\n@@\n `validate-database` checks the revision, every expected count, all declared foreign keys, every unique index, AUTO_INCREMENT positions, transient database-name leakage, the completed marker, and runtime grants. Run `mysqlcheck --check --extended` separately with the migration account. The API smoke command uses FastAPI in-process and covers EOAT, machine, tool, compatibility, Fit Check, history, documents, photos, and home summary reads; it does not bind a network port.\n+\n+Run `verify-empty-baseline` against production immediately before the backup and again after the disposable rehearsal. It requires the exact migrated baseline table counts, absent operational marker, and zero location observations/assertions. After a disposable or production import, attempt the same SQL a second time; the marker guard must refuse it, and a subsequent `validate-database` must still pass.\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction\\docs\\migration\\import_ambiguity_policy.md\n@@\n-- Multiple audited machines do not establish current location. Current location is returned as Unknown / Not Verified.\n+- Multiple audited machines do not establish a lifecycle history. The owner-approved location policy records either proven separate physical units or a cabinet-unspecified `STORED` observed state.\n@@\n-- `26 - Xqual in 25` is preserved verbatim and does not create a machine.\n-- The two conflicting `CL-EOAT-0052` cleanroom rows remain review items and the normalized field remains null.\n+- `26 - Xqual in 25` is preserved verbatim in raw evidence and normalizes to Machine 26.\n+- Proven same-day Cleanroom physical duplicates are split into separately identified EOAT masters using the deterministic mapping in `config/eoat_location_normalization.json`.\n+- `N/A` in the audited machine field means cabinet-unspecified `STORED`; it never creates a cabinet identifier.\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport json, os, subprocess, sys\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom scripts.database.production_data_migration import connect, read_env\nroot=Path.cwd()\nstamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ')\nbaseline=f'eoat_atlas_validation_build_{stamp.lower()}'\noutput=Path(r'C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-operational-packages') / f'corrected-observed-location-{stamp}'\nif output.exists(): raise RuntimeError(f'Output exists: {output}')\nvalues=read_env('development')\nwith connect(values, database=None, migration=True, autocommit=True) as connection:\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(f'CREATE DATABASE `{baseline}` CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci')\n        runtime_user=values.get('EOAT_DB_USER')\n        if not runtime_user: raise RuntimeError('Runtime user is not configured')\n        cursor.execute(f'GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON `{baseline}`.* TO %s@\\'%%\\'', (runtime_user,))\nenvironment=os.environ.copy(); environment.update(values); environment['EOAT_DB_NAME']=baseline\nsubprocess.run([sys.executable,'-m','alembic','-c','server/alembic.ini','upgrade','20260717_0007'],cwd=root,env=environment,check=True)\nsubprocess.run([sys.executable,'scripts/database/build_production_data_migration.py','build','--source-environment','development','--baseline-environment','environment','--output-directory',str(output),'--source-branch','codex/production-location-correction'],cwd=root,env=environment,check=True)\nprint(json.dumps({'baseline_database':baseline,'package_directory':str(output)},indent=2))\n'@ | python -","timeout_ms":180000,"workdir":"C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction"});

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T19:10:21.949Z

The disposable baseline exposed a local privilege setup gap: the migration account could not create or migrate a fresh validation database. I’m granting its required schema-migration privilege only on the disposable database; the runtime account remains restricted to SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport os, subprocess, sys, tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport pymysql\n\nroot = Path.cwd()\npkg = Path(r'C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-operational-packages\\corrected-observed-location-20260720T191243Z')\nbase = Path(os.environ.get('LOCALAPPDATA', Path.home() / 'AppData' / 'Local')) / 'EOAT Atlas Development' / 'database.env'\nsettings = {}\nfor raw in base.read_text(encoding='utf-8-sig').splitlines():\n    line = raw.strip()\n    if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n        key, value = line.split('=', 1)\n        if key.strip().startswith('EOAT_'):\n            settings[key.strip()] = value.strip()\nvalidation_db = 'eoat_atlas_validation_build_20260720t191243z'\nconn = pymysql.connect(host=settings['EOAT_DB_HOST'], port=int(settings.get('EOAT_DB_PORT', '3306')), user=settings['EOAT_DB_USER'], [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT]'EOAT_DB_PASSWORD'], database=validation_db, charset='utf8mb4')\ntry:\n    with conn.cursor() as cur:\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT source_file_path FROM import_batches WHERE status='COMPLETED' AND dry_run=0 ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1\")\n        row = cur.fetchone()\n        if not row or not row[0]:\n            raise RuntimeError('No completed import workbook path is available')\n        workbook = Path(row[0])\nfinally:\n    conn.close()\nif not workbook.is_file():\n    raise RuntimeError(f'Workbook unavailable: {workbook}')\nsettings['EOAT_DB_NAME'] = validation_db\nfd, temp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix='eoat-parity-', EOAT unit.\"\"\"\n    source = _text(source_identifier)\n    row = int(source_row_number)\n    for split in load_policy()[\"physical_unit_splits\"]:\n        if source != split[\"source_identifier\"]:\n            continue\n        for unit in split[\"units\"]:\n            if row in {int(value) for value in unit[\"source_rows\"]}:\n                return units without altering raw evidence.\"\"\"\n    rewritten: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n    for number, row in rows.items():\n        updated = copy.deepcopy(row)\n        source_identifier = _text(updated.get(\"EOAT Assembly ID\"))\n        target_identifier = normalized_eoat_identifier(source_identifier, int(number))\n        if source_identifier != target_identifier:\n external files are reported separately and are not database-loss failures: {summary['external_file_missing']}\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> tuple[int, Path, dict[str, Any]]:\r\n    workbook_path = Path(args.workbook)\r\n    if not workbook_path.is_file():\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Authoritative workbook is unavailable: {workbook_path}\")\r\n    repository_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\r\n    repo = repository_info(repository_root)\r\n    inventory, source_rows, headers = workbook_inventory(workbook_path)\r\n    source_rows = config = load_database_config(args.database_environment)\r\n    database = database_snapshot(config, inventory[\"sha256\"], read_only=args.read_only)\r\n    location_audit = classify_eoat_locations(source_rows.get(\"EOAT Inventory\", {}), database)\r\n    results = reconcile(\r\n        workbook_path, inventory, source_rows, mappings, database, location_audit,\r\n        include_file_existence_check=args.include_file_existence_check,\r\n    )\r\n    summary, verdict = summarize(inventory, mappings, database, results)\r\n\r\n    if args.output_directory:\r\n        output = Path(args.output_directory)\r\n    else:\r\n        stamp = datetime.now().astimezone().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S_%z\")\r\n        output = repository_root / \"reports\" / \"mysql_excel_parity\" / stamp\r\n    output.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=False)\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T19:17:24.151Z

The authoritative source exposed a real parity-tool gap: normalization adds an “Original EOAT Assembly ID” evidence field, but the parity matrix did not map it back to the immutable raw workbook field. I’m correcting that source-level evidence mapping, then I’ll amend the existing corrective commit and rebuild the package so it stays traceable to one final source commit.

{\"14\"}\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_duplicate_source_key_is_reported():\r\n    rows = {\"EOAT Inventory\": {2: {\"Audit ID\": \"AUD-1\"}, 3: {\"Audit ID\": \" aud-1 \"}}, \"Photo Index\": {}}\r\n    duplicates = duplicate_source_keys(rows)\r\n    assert duplicates[0][\"count\"] == 2\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_duplicate_database_key_fixture_is_ambiguous():\r\n    index = _indexed([{\"id\": 1, \"business_identifier\": \"E-1\"}, {\"id\": 2, \"business_identifier\": \"E-1\"}], \"business_identifier\")\r\n    assert len(index[\"e-1\"]) == 2\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_truncated_text_is_not_normalized_away():\r\n    assert comparison(\"complete source value\", \"complete source\") == \"truncated_database_value\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_hyperlink_and_path_normalization():\r\n    assert comparison(r\"folder/sub/file.jpg\", r\"folder\\sub\\file.jpg\", header=\"Folder Path\") == \"normalized_match\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_workbook_cell_comment_is_inventoried(tmp_path):\r\n    path = _save_workbook(tmp_path / \"comment.xlsx\", comment=True)\r\n    inventory, _, _ = workbook_inventory(path)\r\n    assert inventory[\"sheets\"][0][\"comments\"][0][\"text\"] == \"Preserved cell note\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_current_value_changed_after_import_with_history():\r\n    database = {\"audit_log\": [{\r\n        \"id\": 5, \"entity_type\": \"eoat\", \"entity_id\": 9, \"occurred_at\": datetime(2026, 7, 14),\r\n        \"previous_values_json\": {\"notes\": \"old\"}, \"new_values_json\": {\"notes\": \"new\"},\r\n        \"changed_fields_json\": [\"notes\"], \"reason\": \"verified correction\",\r\n    }], \"history\": []}\r\n    assert \"event_id=5\" in find_change_history(database, \"eoat\", 9, \"notes\", \"old\")\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_current_value_changed_without_history_fails_evidence_check():\r\n    assert find_change_history({\"audit_log\": [], \"history\": []}, \"eoat\", 9, \"notes\", \"old\") == \"\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_unmapped_populated_metadata_column_is_failure(tmp_path):\r\n    workbook = Workbook()\r\n    sheet = workbook.active\r\n    sheet.title = \"_EOAT_App_Metadata\"\r\n    sheet.append([\"key\", \"value\"])\r\n    sheet.append([\"schema_version\", \"1.0\"])\r\n    path = tmp_path / \"metadata.xlsx\"\r\n    workbook.save(path)\r\n    _, rows, headers = workbook_inventory(path)\r\n    matrix = mapping_matrix(rows, headers)\r\n    assert {item[\"status\"] for item in matrix} == {\"unmapped_failure\"}\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_hidden_populated_sheet_is_inventoried(tmp_path):\r\n    path = _save_workbook(tmp_path / \"hidden.xlsx\", hidden=True)\r\n    inventory, _, _ = workbook_inventory(path)\r\n    hidden = next(item for item in inventory[\"sheets\"] if item[\"sheet_name\"] == \"Hidden Evidence\")\r\n    assert hidden[\"visibility\"] == \"hidden\"\r\n    assert hidden[\"candidate_data_rows\"] == 1\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_formula_with_cached_value_is_recognized(tmp_path):\r\n    path = _save_workbook(tmp_path / \"cached.xlsx\", formula=\"=1+1\")\r\n    _inject_formula_cache(path)\r\n    inventory, _, _ = workbook_inventory(path)\r\n    sheet = inventory[\"sheets\"][0]\r\n    assert sheet[\"formula_cells\"] == 1\r\n    assert sheet[\"formula_cache_missing\"] == []\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_formula_without_cached_value_is_reported(tmp_path):\r\n    path = _save_workbook(tmp_path / \"uncached.xlsx\", formula=\"=1+1\")\r\n    inventory, _, _ = workbook_inventory(path)\r\n    assert inventory[\"sheets\"][0][\"formula_cache_missing\"] == [\"D2\"]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_malformed_source_value_preserved_in_issue_provenance():\r\n    issue = {\r\n        \"issue_code\": const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport os\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport pymysql\nbase=Path(os.environ.get('LOCALAPPDATA',Path.home()/'AppData'/'Local'))/'EOAT Atlas Development'/'database.env'; s={}\nfor raw in base.read_text(encoding='utf-8-sig').splitlines():\n line=raw.strip()\n if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n  k,v=line.split('=',1)\n  if k.strip().startswith('EOAT_'): s[k.strip()]=v.strip()\ncon=pymysql.connect(host=s['EOAT_DB_HOST'],port=int(s.get('EOAT_DB_PORT','3306')),user=s['EOAT_DB_USER'],password=s['EOAT_DB_PASSWORD'],database='eoat_atlas_dev')\ntry:\n with con.cursor() as cur:\n  cur.execute(\"SELECT issue_code, COUNT(*) FROM import_issues GROUP BY issue_code ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC\")\n  for row in cur.fetchall(): print(*row,sep=' | ')\nfinally: con.close()\n'@ | python -","workdir":"C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction","timeout_ms":10000});

\"created_at\": utcnow(),\r\n        \"frozen_source_directory\": str(target),\r\n        \"originals_are_authoritative\": False,\r\n        \"files\": records,\r\n        \"photo_files\": len(records) - 3,\r\n    }\r\n    write_json(REPORT_ROOT / \"frozen_source_manifest.json\", manifest)\r\n    write_json(target.parent / \"manifest.json\", manifest)\r\n    return manifest\r\n\r\n\r\nISSUE_DISPOSITIONS = {\r\n    \"POSSIBLE_PART_NOT_CONFIRMED\": \"SAFE_TO_DEFER\",\r\n    \"INSTALLATION_DATE_UNKNOWN\": \"DISPLAY_AS_UNKNOWN\",\r\n    \"CURRENT_LOCATION_UNKNOWN\": classify_issues(import_report: Path) -> dict[str, object]:\r\n    report = json.loads(import_report.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\r\n    rows = []\r\n    for issue in report.get(\"issues\", []):\r\n        code = issue[\"issue_code\"]\r\n        disposition = ISSUE_DISPOSITIONS.get(code, \"BLOCKER\")\r\n        rows.append({\r\n            \"issue_id\": issue[\"issue_id\"], \"issue_code\": code, disposition, \"reason\": issue.get(\"current_proposed_action\", \"\"),\r\n                            machine_map.get(_text(row.get(\"Press/Machine #\"))),\r\n                        ),\r\n                    ):\r\n                        if entity:\r\n                            session.add(\r\n                                db.DocumentLink(\r\n                                    document_id=document.id,\r\n                                    entity_type=entity_type,\r\n                                    entity_id=entity.id,\r\n                                    relationship_type=\"legacy_photo\",\r\n                                    is_primary=False,\r\n                                )\r\n                            )\r\n                    import_row = db.ImportRow(\r\n                        import_batch_id=batch.id,\r\n                        source_sheet=\"Photo Index\",\r\n                        source_row_number=number,\r\n                        source_identifier=photo_id,\r\n                        target_entity_type=\"photo\",\r\n                        target_entity_id=photo.id,\r\n                        status=\"IMPORTED\",\r\n                        raw_values_json=raw,\r\n                        normalized_values_json={\"storage_path\": str(candidate)},\r\n                    )\r\n                    session.add(import_row)\r\n                    session.flush()\r\n                    import_rows[(\"Photo Index\", number)] = import_row\r\n                for item in review_items:\r\n                    related_row = import_rows.get((item.source_sheet, item.source_row or -1))\r\n                    session.add(\r\n                        db.ImportIssue(\r\n                            import_batch_id=batch.id,\r\n                            import_row_id=related_row.id if related_row else None,\r\n                            severity=item.severity,\r\n                            issue_code=item.issue_code,\r\n                            field_name=item.field_name or None,\r\n                            source_value=item.source_value or None,\r\n                            description=item.current_proposed_action,\r\n                            suggested_resolution=\"; \".join(item.allowed_resolution_actions),\r\n                            resolution_notes=json.dumps(\r\n                                {\r\n                                    \"issue_id\": item.issue_id,\r\n                                    \"status\": item.resolution_status,\r\n                                    \"related_source_rows\": item.related_source_rows,\r\n                                }\r\n                            ),\r\n                        )\r\n                    )\r\n                counts = {\r\n                    \"plants\": 1,\r\n                    \"areas\": len(area_map),\r\n                    \"eoats\": len(eoat_map),\r\n                    \"machines\": len(machine_map),\r\n                    \"tools\": len(tool_map),\r\n                    \"parts\": 0,\r\n                    \"eoat_machine_compatibility\": len(em_pairs),\r\n                    \"eoat_tool_compatibility\": len(et_pairs),\r\n                    \"tool_machine_compatibility\": len(tm_pairs),\r\n                    \"audit_records\": len(rows[\"EOAT Inventory\"]),\r\n                    \"entity_history_events\": history_event_count,\r\n                    \"documents\": valid_photo_count,\r\n                    \"photos\": valid_photo_count,\r\n                    \"import_rows\": sum(result.rows_discovered_by_sheet.values()),\r\n                    \"import_issues\": len(review_items),\r\n                    \"eoat_installations\": 0,\r\n                }\r\n                batch.status = \"COMPLETED\"\r\n                batch.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\r\n                batch.records_imported = sum(counts.values())\r\n                batch.records_rejected = 0\r\n                batch.warnings_count = len(review_items)\r\n const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport os,json\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport pymysql\nbase=Path(os.environ.get('LOCALAPPDATA',Path.home()/'AppData'/'Local'))/'EOAT Atlas Development'/'database.env'; s={}\nfor raw in base.read_text(encoding='utf-8-sig').splitlines():\n line=raw.strip()\n if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n  k,v=line.split('=',1)\n  if k.strip().startswith('EOAT_'):s[k.strip()]=v.strip()\ncon=pymysql.connect(host=s['EOAT_DB_HOST'],port=int(s.get('EOAT_DB_PORT','3306')),user=s['EOAT_DB_USER'],password=s['EOAT_DB_PASSWORD'],database='eoat_atlas_dev',cursorclass=pymysql.cursors.DictCursor)\ntry:\n with con.cursor() as cur:\n  cur.execute(\"SELECT issue_code,field_name,source_value,description,suggested_resolution,resolution_notes FROM import_issues WHERE issue_code IN ('CONFLICTING_EOAT_ATTRIBUTE','MISSING_TOOL','PLACEHOLDER_PHOTO_ROW','CONFLICTING_CURRENT_ASSIGNMENT') ORDER BY id\")\n  for r in cur.fetchall():\n   r['resolution_notes']=json.loads(r['resolution_notes']) if r['resolution_notes'] else {}\n   print(json.dumps(r,ensure_ascii=False))\nfinally:con.close()\n'@ | python -","workdir":"C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction","timeout_ms":10000});
text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));

json\nimport sys\nfrom collections import Counter\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nREPOSITORY_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\nif str(REPOSITORY_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n return value\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return json.loads(value)\n    return {}\n\n\ndef _source_rows(connection) -> tuple[dict[int, dict[str, Any]], str, str]:\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT source_row_number,raw_values_json,import_batch_id FROM import_rows \"\n            \"WHERE source_sheet=%s ORDER BY source_row_number\",\n            (WORKSHEET,),\n        )\n        imported = cursor.fetchall()\n        rows = {int(row[\"source_row_number\"]): _json(row[\"raw_values_json\"]) for row in imported}\n        batch_ids = {int(row[\"import_batch_id\"]) for row in imported}\n        if len(batch_ids) != 1:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"EOAT Inventory source rows must belong to one import batch\")\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT source_file_name,source_file_checksum FROM import_batches WHERE id=%s\",\n            (next(iter(batch_ids)),),\n        )\n        batch = cursor.fetchone()\n    if not rows or not batch or not batch[\"source_file_checksum\"]:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Authoritative EOAT workbook evidence is unavailable from import provenance\")\n    return rows, str(batch[\"source_file_checksum\"]), Path(str(batch[\"source_file_name\"])).name\n\n\ndef _columns(connection, table: str) -> list[str]:\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT column_name,extra FROM information_schema.columns \"\n            \"WHERE table_schema=DATABASE() AND table_name=%s ORDER BY ordinal_position\",\n -> int:\n    columns = list(row)\n    values = [row[column] for column in columns]\n    quoted = \",\".join(f\"`{column}`\" for column in columns)\n    placeholders = \",\".join([\"%s\"] * len(columns))\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(f\"INSERT INTO `{table}` ({quoted}) VALUES ({placeholders})\", values)\n        return int(cursor.lastrowid)\n\n\ndef _append_provenance(existing: Any, message: str) -> str:\n    text = \"\" if existing is None else str(existing).strip()\n    if PROVENANCE_MARKER in text:\n        return text\n    return f\"{text}\\n\\n{message}\".strip()\n\n\ndef _eoat_id(connection, identifier: str) -> int:\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT id FROM eoats WHERE business_identifier=%s\", (identifier,))\n        row = cursor.fetchone()\n    if not row:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"EOAT identifier is missing: {identifier}\")\n    return int(row[\"id\"])\n\n\ndef _clone_eoat(connection, source_identifier: str, target_identifier: str, source_rows: list[int]) -> int:\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT * FROM eoats WHERE business_identifier=%s\", (source_identifier,))\n        source = cursor.fetchone()\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT id FROM eoats WHERE business_identifier=%s\", (target_identifier,))\n        existing = cursor.fetchone()\n    if not source:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Physical-unit split source EOAT is missing: {source_identifier}\")\n    provenance = (\n        f\"{PROVENANCE_MARKER}: split from shared workbook identifier {source_identifier}; \"\n        f\"this unit is supported by EOAT Inventory row(s) {', '.join(str(row) for row in source_rows)}. \"\n        \"Compatibility relationships are shared evidence; audit, document, photo, and lifecycle history were not copied.\"\n    )\n    if existing:\n        return int(existing[\"id\"])\n    copied = {column: source[column] for column in _columns(connection, \"eoats\") if column != \"id\"}\n    copied.update({\n        \"business_identifier\": target_identifier,\n        \"legacy_identifier\": source_identifier,\n        \"display_name\": target_identifier,\n        \"notes\": (\n            str(source.get(\"notes\") or \"\").strip()\n NORMALIZATION_SOURCE,\n        \"row_version\": 1,\n    })\n    return _insert(connection, \"eoats\", copied)\n\n\ndef _copy_shared_compatibility(connection, source_eoat_id: int, target_eoat_id: int) -> dict[str, int]:\n    copied_counts: dict[str, int] = {}\n    for table in (\"eoat_machine_compatibility\", \"eoat_tool_compatibility\"):\n        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(f\"SELECT * FROM `{table}` WHERE eoat_id=%s ORDER BY id\", (source_eoat_id,))\n            rows = cursor.fetchall()\n            cursor.execute(f\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM `{table}` WHERE eoat_id=%s\", (target_eoat_id,))\n            existing_count = int(cursor.fetchone()[\"count\"])\n        if existing_count not in {0, len(rows)}:\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"{table} has a partial prior copy for EOAT id {target_eoat_id}\")\n        if existing_count == len(rows):\n            copied_counts[table] = 0\n            continue\n        for row in rows:\n            copied = {column: row[column] for column in _columns(connection, table) if column != \"id\"}\n            copied[\"eoat_id\"] = target_eoat_id\n            copied[\"source_system\"] = NORMALIZATION_SOURCE\n            copied[\"row_version\"] = 1\n            _insert(connection, table, copied)\n        copied_counts[table] = len(rows)\n    return copied_counts\n\n\ndef _reassign_audit_rows(connection, source_identifier: str, target_identifier: str, source_rows: list[int]) -> None:\n    source_eoat_id = _eoat_id(connection, source_identifier)\n    target_eoat_id = _eoat_id(connection, target_identifier)\n    for source_row in source_rows:\n        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"SELECT id,eoat_id FROM audit_records WHERE source_sheet=%s AND source_row_number=%s\",\n                (WORKSHEET, int(source_row)),\n            )\n            audit = cursor.fetchone()\n        if not audit:\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"Missing audit record for EOAT Inventory row {source_row}\")\n        if int(audit[\"eoat_id\"]) not in {source_eoat_id, target_eoat_id}:\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"Audit row {source_row} is linked to an unexpected EOAT\")\n        if int(audit[\"eoat_id\"]) != target_eoat_id:\n            with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n                cursor.execute(\"UPDATE audit_records SET eoat_id=%s,row_version=row_version+1 WHERE id=%s\", (target_eoat_id, audit[\"id\"]))\n\n\ndef _apply_physical_unit_splits(connection, policy: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    report: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    for split in policy[\"physical_unit_splits\"]:\n        source_identifier = str(split[\"source_identifier\"])\n f\"{PROVENANCE_MARKER}: shared workbook identifier split into physical units \"\n            f\"{', '.join(unit_identifiers)}. {split['reason']}\"\n        )\n        existing_notes = _clone_eoat(connection, source_identifier, target_identifier, list(unit[\"source_rows\"]))\n            if target_identifier != source_identifier:\n        report.append({\n            \"source_identifier\": source_identifier,\n            \"normalized_unit_identifiers\": unit_identifiers,\n            \"reason\": split[\"reason\"],\n cursor.execute(\"SELECT notes FROM eoats WHERE id=%s\", (eoat_id,))\n        row = cursor.fetchone()\n    return \"\" if not row or row[\"notes\"] is None else str(row[\"notes\"])\n\n\ndef normalize_machine_reference(row.get(\"Press/Machine #\"))\n        raw = str(row.get(\"Press/Machine #\") or \"\").strip()\n        if not canonical or raw == canonical:\n            continue\n             \"WHERE source_sheet=%s AND source_row_number=%s\",\n                (canonical, WORKSHEET, source_row),\n            )\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"UPDATE audit_records SET machine_id=%s,row_version=row_version+1 \"\n                \"WHERE source_sheet=%s AND source_row_number=%s\",\n                (machine_id, WORKSHEET, source_row),\n            )\n        applied.append({\"source_row_number\": source_row, \"original_machine\": raw, \"normalized_machine\": canonical})\n    return applied\n\n\ndef _replace_location_evidence(connection, rows: dict[int, dict[str, Any]], digest: str, workbook_name: str, policy: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    plan = build_plan(\n        normalized_source_rows(rows), load_database(connection),\n        workbook_sha256=digest, source_workbook=workbook_name,\n    )\n    if plan[\"state_counts\"] != policy[\"expected_location_state_counts\"]:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"Owner-approved location totals do not match: {plan['state_counts']} != {policy['expected_location_state_counts']}\"\n        )\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n        cursor.execute(\"DELETE FROM eoat_location_assertions\")\n        cursor.execute(\"DELETE FROM eoat_location_observations\")\n    apply_plan(connection, plan, commit=False)\n    return plan\n\n\ndef correct(connection, *, apply: bool) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    policy = load_policy()\n    identity = database_identity(connection)\n    assert_safe_source(identity)\n    if identity[\"alembic_revision\"] != REQUIRED_REVISION:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Schema mismatch: required {REQUIRED_REVISION}, found {identity['alembic_revision']}\")\n    rows, digest, workbook_name = _source_rows(connection)\n    if not apply:\n        planned_rows = normalized_source_rows(rows)\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"PLANNED\",\n            \"database\": identity[\"database_name\"],\n            \"source_workbook\": workbook_name,\n            \"source_workbook_sha256\": digest,\n            \"approval\": owner_approval_evidence(),\n            \"physical_unit_splits\": policy[\"physical_unit_splits\"],\n            \"planned_source_row_targets\": {\n                str(number): target[\"EOAT Assembly ID\"] for number, target in planned_rows.items()\n                if target.get(\"Original EOAT Assembly ID\")\n            },\n        }\n    try:\n        aliases = _apply_machine_aliases(connection, rows)\n        split_report = _apply_physical_unit_splits(connection, policy)\n        plan = _replace_location_evidence(connection, rows, digest, workbook_name, policy)\n        connection.commit()\n    except Exception:\n        connection.rollback()\n        raise\n    return {\n        \"status\": \"APPLIED\",\n        \"database\": identity[\"database_name\"],\n        \"source_workbook\": workbook_name,\n        \"source_workbook_sha256\": digest,\n        \"approval\": owner_approval_evidence(),\n        \"machine_aliases_applied\": aliases,\n        \"physical_unit_splits\": split_report,\n        \"location_observation_count\": len(plan[\"observations\"]),\n        \"location_assertion_count\": len(plan[\"assertions\"]),\n        \"location_state_counts\": plan[\"state_counts\"],\n    }\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Apply owner-approved EOAT observed-location normalization to development\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--environment\", default=\"development\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--apply\", action=\"store_true\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--report\", type=Path, required=True)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    from scripts.database.production_data_migration import read_env\n\n    values = read_env(args.environment)\n    with connect(values) as connection:\n        report = correct(connection, apply=args.apply)\n    args.report.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    write_json(args.report, report)\n    print(json.dumps({\"status\": report[\"status\"], \"report\": str(args.report)}, indent=2))\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.7 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.7 seconds\nOutput:\n# Production operational-data migration\r\n\r\nEOAT Atlas production operational data is promoted with `scripts/database/build_production_data_migration.py`. It is not promoted with an all-table `mysqldump` because a newly migrated production database already contains Alembic-owned lookup rows and because development authentication/runtime state is not portable.\r\n\r\n## Required invariants\r\n\r\n- The source must be the authoritative development MySQL database and must be at Alembic revision `20260717_0007`.\r\n- The production-equivalent baseline must be a newly migrated disposable MySQL database at the same revision.\r\n- A production database, a name ending in `_prod`, or a test database outside explicit validation mode is rejected as an export source.\r\n- All 53 base tables must have a policy in `TABLE_POLICIES`; an unknown or missing table stops the build.\r\n- Alembic-seeded rows are reconciled by stable code. Meaning conflicts stop the build. Source-only operational codes are assigned deterministic non-colliding target IDs, and dependent foreign keys are remapped by meaning.\r\n- Development users, roles assignments, sessions, authentication audit events, application instances/releases, cutover sessions, idempotency state, external group mappings, and environment settings are excluded.\r\n- Completed sanctioned `import_batches`, `import_rows`, and `import_issues` are retained because operational rows reference them as provenance.\r\n- Generated columns are never exported. Binary values use MySQL hexadecimal literals, text is UTF-8/utf8mb4, and rows are ordered by primary key.\r\n- The import is accepted only against exact migrated-baseline counts. A persistent `system_metadata` marker is written as `IN_PROGRESS` before the transactional data load and changed to `COMPLETED` on success. Either state prevents an accidental second import.\r\n\r\n## Build\r\n\r\nCreate and migrate a disposable baseline first. Load connection values from an untracked environment file; never place passwords on the command line.\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npy -3.14 -m alembic -c server/alembic.ini upgrade 20260717_0007\r\npy -3.14 scripts/database/build_production_data_migration.py build `\r\n  --source-environment development `\r\n  --baseline-environment C:\\secure\\baseline.env `\r\n  --source-branch development/mysql-api-consolidated `\r\n  --output-directory C:\\migration-evidence\\eoat-operational-data\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe output contains the SQL artifact and checksum, manifest, all-table classification, source/expected counts, seed parity, exclusions and transformations, file-reference analysis, validation report, a standalone copy of the migration utility, and controlled import/rollback instructions.\r\n\r\nFor the approved observed-location correction, the package also contains the owner approval evidence, required empty-production baseline counts, location observation/assertion report, duplicate-resolution report, and an explicit supersession record for `eoat-operational-633d0596386fc44b33c2`. The superseded package must never be amended or overwritten.\r\n\r\n## Disposable validation\r\n\r\nMigrate a second empty database, import `operational-data.sql`, and run both validation commands without starting a live API:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npy -3.14 scripts/database/build_production_data_migration.py validate-database `\r\n  --package-directory C:\\migration-evidence\\eoat-operational-data `\r\n  --database-environment C:\\secure\\validation.env\r\npy -3.14 scripts/database/build_production_data_migration.py api-smoke `\r\n  --package-directory C:\\migration-evidence\\eoat-operational-data `\r\n  --database-environment C:\\secure\\validation.env\r\n```\r\n\r\n`validate-database` checks the revision, every expected count, all declared foreign keys, every unique index, AUTO_INCREMENT positions, transient database-name leakage, the completed marker, and runtime grants. Run `mysqlcheck --check --extended` separately with the migration account. The API smoke command uses FastAPI in-process and covers EOAT, machine, tool, compatibility, Fit Check, history, documents, photos, and home summary reads; it does not bind a network port.\r\n\r\nRun `verify-empty-baseline` against production immediately before the backup and again after the disposable rehearsal. It requires the exact migrated baseline table counts, absent operational marker, and zero location observations/assertions. After a disposable or production import, attempt the same SQL a second time; the marker guard must refuse it, and a subsequent `validate-database` must still pass.\r\n\r\nRun `tools/verify_master_tracker_mysql_parity.py` against the imported disposable database. The verifier is state-aware: compatibility rows never imply current installation; explicit cabinet/not-installed audit notes override a generic machine/context field; and conflicting simultaneous machine observations must be represented by `CONFLICTING` observations. It writes `eoat_location_state.csv` and `state_aware_location_parity.json` in addition to the general parity evidence.\r\n\r\nPackages built for `20260715_0006` are obsolete and must be rejected. Use only a new migration identity whose manifest requires `20260717_0007`; never amend or reuse an older package.\r\n\r\nAn observed current installation is not installation history. Revision `20260717_0007` represents this evidence in `eoat_location_observations` and retains row-level support in `eoat_location_assertions`. Date-only audits use `observed_on` with no fabricated `observed_at`. Real lifecycle tables still require genuine event times, and storage assignments still require a real storage location. â€œEOAT in cabinetâ€ may prove stored state without identifying a cabinet; keep the observation target null rather than inventing a cabinet code.\r\n\r\nA failing strict parity verdict is a go/no-go blocker even when the data artifact perfectly reproduces the authoritative MySQL source. Preserve the detailed evidence instead of converting unresolved source issues into â€œexpectedâ€ differences.\r\n\r\n## Production and rollback\r\n\r\nUse the generated `IMPORT_INSTRUCTIONS.md` and `ROLLBACK_INSTRUCTIONS.md`. They require checksums, a full pre-import backup, restore validation in a disposable database, exact typed confirmations, a disposable import before production, post-import validation and backup, and preservation of failed-import evidence. The procedure does not start the API and does not modify systemd or Nginx.\r\n- Credential/secret scan: PASS\r\n- Development authentication/session data excluded: PASS\r\n- File references inspected: {file_report['total_document_references']}\r\n- Owner-approved location normalization evidence: `{APPROVAL_EVIDENCE_FILENAME}` (PASS)\r\n- Duplicate/location resolution report: `{DUPLICATE_RESOLUTION_FILENAME}` (PASS)\r\n- Superseded migration: `{SUPERSEDED_OPERATIONAL_MIGRATION_ID}`\r\n\r\nDisposable import, integrity, parity, and API read-smoke results are written by `migration_tool.py validate-database`.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n        (output / \"validation-report.md\").write_text(validation_md, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n        write_instructions(output, manifest)\r\n    print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"PASS\", \"output_directory\": str(output), \"sha256\": digest}, indent=2))\r\n    return 0\r\n\r\n\r\ndef write_instructions(output: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\r\n    migration_id = manifest[\"migration_id\"]\r\n    digest = manifest[\"artifact_sha256\"]\r\n    release_evidence_dir = str(manifest.get(\"source_git\", {}).get(\"commit\") or \"uncommitted\")[:7]\r\n    import_text = f\"\"\"# EOAT Atlas controlled operational-data import\r\n\r\nThese commands are documentation only. Run them interactively on EOAT-ATLAS as an authorized operator. The database login paths must be created beforehand with `mysql_config_editor`; never place passwords on a command line.\r\n\r\n```bash\r\nset -euo pipefail\r\nPACKAGE=/opt/eoat-atlas/incoming/{migration_id}\r\ncd \"$PACKAGE\"\r\nsha256sum -c operational-data.sql.sha256\r\n/opt/eoat-atlas/current/venv/bin/python migration_tool.py verify-package \"{REQUIRED_REVISION}\"\r\nEOAT_DB_NAME=eoat_atlas_prod /opt/eoat-atlas/current/venv/bin/python migration_tool.py verify-empty-baseline --package-directory \"$PACKAGE\"\r\n\r\nSTAMP=$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)\r\nBACKUP=/opt/eoat-atlas/shared/backups/eoat_atlas_prod-pre-operational-import-$STAMP.sql\r\nmysqldump --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --single-transaction --no-tablespaces \"$BACKUP\"\r\n\r\nVALIDATION_DB=eoat_atlas_validation_{migration_id[-8:]}\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin -e \"CREATE DATABASE \\\\`$VALIDATION_DB\\\\` CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci\"\r\nEOAT_DB_NAME=\"$VALIDATION_DB\" /opt/eoat-atlas/current/venv/bin/python -m alembic -c /opt/eoat-atlas/current/server/alembic.ini upgrade {REQUIRED_REVISION}\r\nmysql \"$PACKAGE\"\r\nmysqlcheck --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --check --extended \"$VALIDATION_DB\"\r\nif mysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin \"$VALIDATION_DB\" < operational-data.sql; then\r\n  echo 'ERROR: duplicate import unexpectedly succeeded' >&2\r\n  exit 1\r\nfi\r\necho 'PASS: duplicate import correctly refused'\r\nEOAT_DB_NAME=\"$VALIDATION_DB\" /opt/eoat-atlas/current/venv/bin/python migration_tool.py validate-database --package-directory \"$PACKAGE\"\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin -e \"DROP DATABASE \\\\`$VALIDATION_DB\\\\`\"\r\n\r\n# Owner-approved normalization evidence is verified by verify-package above.\r\ntest -s \"$PACKAGE/{APPROVAL_EVIDENCE_FILENAME}\"\r\ntest -s operational-data.sql | awk '{{print $1}}')\" = \"{digest}\"\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin eoat_atlas_prod < operational-data.sql\r\nEOAT_DB_NAME=eoat_atlas_prod The persistent marker `{manifest['import_marker']}` and exact baseline-count guard refuse duplicate or non-baseline imports. An `IN_PROGRESS` marker indicates an interrupted attempt and requires evidence preservation plus rollback, not a blind retry.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n    rollback_text = f\"\"\"# EOAT Atlas controlled rollback\r\n\r\nUse the exact pre-import backup created by the import procedure. Never delete or overwrite it.\r\n\r\n```bash\r\nset -euo pipefail\r\nBACKUP=/opt/eoat-atlas/shared/backups/eoat_atlas_prod-pre-operational-import-REPLACE_WITH_STAMP.sql\r\ntest -s \"$BACKUP\"\r\ntest -s \"$BACKUP.sha256\"\r\ncd \"$(dirname \"$BACKUP\")\"\r\nsha256sum -c \"$(basename \"$BACKUP\").sha256\"\r\n\r\nRESTORE_CHECK=eoat_atlas_restore_check_{migration_id[-8:]}\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin -e \"CREATE DATABASE \\\\`$RESTORE_CHECK\\\\` CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci\"\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin \"$RESTORE_CHECK\" < \"$BACKUP\"\r\ntest \"$(mysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --batch --skip-column-names \"$RESTORE_CHECK\" -e 'SELECT version_num FROM alembic_version')\" = \"{REQUIRED_REVISION}\"\r\nmysqlcheck --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --check --extended \"$RESTORE_CHECK\"\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin -e \"DROP DATABASE \\\\`$RESTORE_CHECK\\\\`\"\r\n\r\nEXPECTED='RESTORE eoat_atlas_prod FROM VERIFIED PRE-IMPORT BACKUP'\r\nread -r -p \"Type exactly: $EXPECTED: \" CONFIRM\r\ntest \"$CONFIRM\" = \"$EXPECTED\"\r\nFAILED=/opt/eoat-atlas/shared/backups/eoat_atlas_prod-failed-import-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ).sql\r\nmysqldump --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --single-transaction --no-tablespaces --routines --triggers --events --hex-blob --set-gtid-purged=OFF eoat_atlas_prod > \"$FAILED\"\r\nsha256sum \"$FAILED\" > \"$FAILED.sha256\"\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin -e 'DROP DATABASE eoat_atlas_prod; CREATE DATABASE eoat_atlas_prod CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci'\r\nmysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin eoat_atlas_prod < \"$BACKUP\"\r\ntest \"$(mysql --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --batch --skip-column-names eoat_atlas_prod -e 'SELECT version_num FROM alembic_version')\" = \"{REQUIRED_REVISION}\"\r\nmysqlcheck --login-path=eoat-atlas-prod-admin --check --extended eoat_atlas_prod\r\n```\r\n\r\nStop immediately if the backup or checksum is absent or invalid. Preserve the failed-state dump, package, validation reports, and logs.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n    (output / \"IMPORT_INSTRUCTIONS.md\").write_text(import_text, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n    (output / \"ROLLBACK_INSTRUCTIONS.md\").write_text(rollback_text, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\n\r\ndef verify_package(package: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\r\n    manifest = json.loads((package / \"migration-manifest.json\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\r\n    artifact = package / manifest[\"artifact_filename\"]\r\n    actual = hashlib.sha256(artifact.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\r\n    if actual != manifest[\"artifact_sha256\"]:\r\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Artifact checksum mismatch: expected {manifest['artifact_sha256']}, actual {actual}\")\r\n    if manifest[\"required_production_schema_revision\"] != REQUIRED_REVISION:\r\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Manifest target revision is not approved\")\r\n    if secret_findings(artifact.read_bytes()):\r\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Artifact secret scan failed\")\r\n    if manifest.get(\"supersedes_operational_migration_id\") != SUPERSEDED_OPERATIONAL_MIGRATION_ID:\r\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Package does not explicitly supersede the rejected migration\")\r\n    for key, filename in (\r\n        (\"required_empty_production_baseline_counts_file\", EMPTY_BASELINE_FILENAME),\r\n        (\"owner_approval_evidence_file\", APPROVAL_EVIDENCE_FILENAME),\r\n        (\"duplicate_resolution_report_file\", DUPLICATE_RESOLUTION_FILENAME),\r\n    ):\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

host=values.get(\"EOAT_DB_HOST\", \"127.0.0.1\"),\r\n        port=int(values.get(\"EOAT_DB_PORT\", \"3306\")),\r\n        user=values.get(f\"{prefix}USER\", values.get(\"EOAT_DB_USER\", \"\")),\r\n connection.cursor() as cursor:\r\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT VERSION() server_version, DATABASE() database_name, \"\r\n                       \"(SELECT version_num FROM alembic_version LIMIT 1) alembic_revision\")\r\n        result = cursor.fetchone()\r\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT @@hostname server_hostname, @@port server_port\")\r\n        result.update(cursor.fetchone())\r\n    return result\r\n\r\n\r\ndef assert_safe_source(identity: dict[str, Any], *, validation_mode: bool = False) -> None:\r\n    name = str(identity[\"database_name\"]).casefold()\r\n    if name in PRODUCTION_NAMES or name.endswith(\"_prod\"):\r\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Refusing production database '{identity['database_name']}' as an export source\")\r\n    if any(marker in name for marker in TEST_NAME_MARKERS) and not validation_mode:\r\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Refusing test database '{identity['database_name']}' outside validation mode\")\r\n    if identity[\"alembic_revision\"] != REQUIRED_REVISION:\r\n        raise RuntimeError(\r\n            f\"Source schema mismatch: required {REQUIRED_REVISION}, found {identity['alembic_revision']}\"\r\n        )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef table_names(connection) -> list[str]:\r\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\r\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables \"\r\n                       \"WHERE table_schema=DATABASE() AND table_type='BASE TABLE' ORDER BY table_name\")\r\n        return [row[\"TABLE_NAME\"] for row in cursor.fetchall()]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef row_counts(connection) -> dict[str, int]:\r\n    result: dict[str, int] = {}\r\n    with connection.cursor() as cursor:\r\n        for table in table_names(connection):\r\n    output = Path(args.output_directory).resolve()\r\n    if output.exists():\r\n        raise FileExistsError(f\"Output directory already exists: {output}\")\r\n    source_values = read_env(args.source_environment)\r\n    baseline_values = read_env(args.baseline_environment)\r\n    with connect(source_values) as source, connect(baseline_values) as baseline:\r\n        source_identity = database_identity(source)\r\n        baseline_identity = database_identity(baseline)\r\n        assert_safe_source(source_identity, validation_mode=args.validation_mode)\r\n        if baseline_identity[\"alembic_revision\"] != REQUIRED_REVISION:\r\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"Baseline revision mismatch: {baseline_identity['alembic_revision']}\")\r\n        source_tables = table_names(source)\r\n        baseline_tables = table_names(baseline)\r\n        if source_tables != baseline_tables:\r\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Source and disposable baseline table sets differ\")\r\n        if set(source_tables) != set(TABLE_POLICIES):\r\n            missing = sorted(set(source_tables) - set(TABLE_POLICIES))\r\n            extra = sorted(set(TABLE_POLICIES) - set(source_tables))\r\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"Classification is incomplete; missing policies={missing}, stale policies={extra}\")\r\n        source_meta = schema_metadata(source)\r\n        source_counts = row_counts(source)\r\n TABLE_POLICIES.items() if policy.copy and table not in SEED_TABLE_KEYS}\r\n        order = list(SEED_TABLE_KEYS) + dependency_order(source_meta, included)\r\n        export_rows: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}\r\n        transformation_report: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {}\r\n        exported_counts: dict[str, int] = {}\r\n        for table in order:\r\n            if table in SEED_TABLE_KEYS:\r\n                rows = seed_additions[table]\r\n                transforms = {}\r\n            else:\r\n                rows, transforms = transform_rows(\r\n                    table, fetch_rows(source, table, source_meta[table]), source_meta[table], seed_maps\r\n                )\r\n            export_rows[table] = rows\r\n            exported_counts[table] = len(rows)\r\n            if transforms:\r\n                transformation_report[table] = transforms\r\n        expected_counts = dict(baseline_counts)\r\n        for table in SEED_TABLE_KEYS:\r\n            expected_counts[table] += len(seed_additions[table])\r\n        for table in included:\r\n            expected_counts[table] = source_counts[table]\r\n        expected_counts[\"system_metadata\"] = baseline_counts[\"system_metadata\"] + 1\r\n        repo = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\r\n        git = git_info(repo)\r\n        if args.source_branch:\r\n            branch_commit = subprocess.check_output(\r\n                [\"git\", \"-C\", str(repo), \"rev-parse\", args.source_branch], text=True\r\n            ).strip()\r\n            if branch_commit != git[\"commit\"]:\r\n source_identity[\"database_name\"], \"revision\": source_identity[\"alembic_revision\"]},\r\n            \"git\": git,\r\n            \"counts\": source_counts,\r\n        }, sort_keys=True).encode()\r\n        migration_id = \"eoat-operational-\" + hashlib.sha256(snapshot_payload).hexdigest()[:20]\r\n        marker_key = cursor.execute(\r\n            \"SELECT e.business_identifier,m.machine_number,t.tool_number \"\r\n            \"FROM eoat_machine_compatibility em JOIN eoats e ON e.id=em.eoat_id \"\r\n            \"JOIN machines m ON m.id=em.machine_id \"\r\n            \"JOIN tool_machine_compatibility tm ON tm.machine_id=m.id JOIN tools t ON t.id=tm.tool_id \"\r\n            \"JOIN eoat_tool_compatibility et ON et.eoat_id=e.id AND et.tool_id=t.id \"\r\n            \"ORDER BY e.id,m.id,t.id LIMIT 1\"\r\n        )\r\n        triple_row = cursor.fetchone()\r\n    triple = (\r\n TestClient\r\n\r\n    from server.eoat_api.app import app\r\n\r\n    checks: dict[str, int] = {}\r\n    with TestClient(app) as client:\r\n        requests = {\r\n            \"health\": (\"get\", \"/api/v1/health\", None),\r\n            \"eoat_list\": (\"get\", \"/api/v1/eoats\", {\"limit\": 5}),\r\n            \"eoat_detail\": (\"get\", f\"/api/v1/eoats/{eoat}\", None),\r\n            \"eoat_current_location\": (\"get\", f\"/api/v1/eoats/{eoat}/current-location\", None),\r\n            \"eoat_location_observations\": (\"get\", f\"/api/v1/eoats/{eoat}/location-observations\",
