Path(args.package_directory).resolve()\r\n    verify_package(package)\r\n    expected = json.loads((package / \"expected-production-row-counts.json\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\r\n    values = read_env(args.database_environment)\r\n    with connect(values) as connection:\r\n        identity = database_identity(connection)\r\n        if identity[\"alembic_revision\"] != REQUIRED_REVISION:\r\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"Validation database revision mismatch: {identity['alembic_revision']}\")\r\n        actual = row_counts(connection)\r\n        mismatches = {table: {\"expected\": count, \"actual\": actual.get(table)}\r\n                      for table, count in expected.items() if actual.get(table) != count}\r\n        orphans = orphan_checks(connection)\r\n        duplicates = duplicate_checks(connection)\r\n        auto = auto_increment_checks(connection)\r\n        transient = transient_name_checks(connection)\r\n        grants = runtime_grant_check(connection)\r\n        marker = json.loads((package / \"migration-manifest.json\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))[\"import_marker\"]\r\n        with connection.cursor() as cursor:\r\n            cursor.execute(\"SELECT metadata_value FROM system_metadata WHERE metadata_key=%s\", (marker,))\r\n            marker_row = cursor.fetchone()\r\n        status = \"PASS\" if (\r\n            not mismatches and not any(x[\"orphan_count\"] for x in orphans)\r\n            and not any(x[\"duplicate_groups\"] for x in duplicates)\r\n            and all(x[\"valid\"] for x in auto) and not transient\r\n            and grants[\"valid\"]\r\n            and marker_row and marker_row[\"metadata_value\"] == \"COMPLETED\"\r\n        ) else \"FAIL\"\r\n        report = {\r\n            \"status\": status,\r\n            \"database\": {\"host\": values.get(\"EOAT_DB_HOST\", \"127.0.0.1\"),\r\n                         \"port\": int(values.get(\"EOAT_DB_PORT\", \"3306\")),\r\n                         \"name\": identity[\"database_name\"], \"server_version\": identity[\"server_version\"],\r\n                         \"alembic_revision\": identity[\"alembic_revision\"]},\r\n            \"row_count_mismatches\": mismatches,\r\n            \"actual_row_counts\": actual,\r\n            \"foreign_key_orphan_checks\": orphans,\r\n            \"unique_key_duplicate_checks\": duplicates,\r\n            \"auto_increment_checks\": auto,\r\n            \"transient_database_name_findings\": transient,\r\n            \"runtime_permission_check\": grants,\r\n            \"import_marker_complete\": bool(marker_row and marker_row[\"metadata_value\"] == \"COMPLETED\"),\r\n            \"validated_at_utc\": utc_now(),\r\n        }\r\n    write_json(package / \"disposable-validation-report.json\", report)\r\n    with (package / \"validation-report.md\").open(\"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as stream:\r\n        stream.write(f\"\\n## Disposable import validation\\n\\n- Result: **{status}**\\n\")\r\n        stream.write(f\"- Row-count mismatches: {len(mismatches)}\\n\")\r\n        stream.write(f\"- Foreign-key orphans: {sum(x['orphan_count'] for x in orphans)}\\n\")\r\n        stream.write(f\"- Duplicate unique-key groups: {sum(x['duplicate_groups'] for x in duplicates)}\\n\")\r\n        stream.write(f\"- AUTO_INCREMENT failures: {sum(not x['valid'] for x in auto)}\\n\")\r\n        stream.write(f\"- Transient database-name findings: {len(transient)}\\n\")\r\n    print(json.dumps({\"status\": status, \"report\": str(package / escape(value):\r\n        if isinstance(value, str):\r\n            return \"'\" + value.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace(\"'\", \"\\\\'\") + \"'\"\r\n        return str(value)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_table_classification_is_complete_for_all_55_tables() -> None:\r\n    assert len(EXPECTED_TABLES) == 55\r\n    assert set(migration.TABLE_POLICIES) == EXPECTED_TABLES\r\n    assert {policy.group for policy in migration.TABLE_POLICIES.values()} == {\"A\", \"B\", \"C\", \"D\", \"E\"}\r\n    assert all(policy.copy != policy.exclude for policy in migration.TABLE_POLICIES.values())\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_seed_policy_uses_stable_key_merge_and_never_blind_copy() -> None:\r\n    assert set(migration.SEED_TABLE_KEYS) == {\r\n        \"asset_statuses\", \"compatibility_sources\", \"compatibility_statuses\",\r\n        \"document_types\", \"history_event_types\", \"roles\",\r\n    }\r\n    for table in migration.SEED_TABLE_KEYS:\r\n        policy = migration.TABLE_POLICIES[table]\r\n        assert policy.merge_seeded_rows\r\n        assert not policy.preserve_ids\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_seed_meaning_distinguishes_metadata_drift_from_conflict() -> None:\r\n    source = {\"code\": \"photo\", \"display_name\": \"Photo\", \"description\": \"Imported\", \"sort_order\": 0, \"is_active\": 1}\r\n    baseline = {\"code\": \"photo\", \"display_name\": \"Photo\", \"description\": \"Controlled\", \"sort_order\": 20, \"is_active\": 1}\r\n    assert migration.seed_meaning(source, \"code\") == migration.seed_meaning(baseline, \"code\")\r\n    assert migration.semantic_row(source) != migration.semantic_row(baseline)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_source_schema_mismatch_is_rejected() -> None:\r\n    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match=\"schema mismatch\"):\r\n        migration.assert_safe_source({\"database_name\": \"eoat_atlas_dev\", \"alembic_revision\": \"old\"})\r\n\r\n\r\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\"name\", [\"eoat_atlas_prod\", \"EOAT_ATLAS_PROD\", \"customer_prod\"])\r\ndef test_production_source_is_rejected(name: str) -> None:\r\n    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match=\"production\"):\r\n        migration.assert_safe_source({\"database_name\": name, \"alembic_revision\": migration.REQUIRED_REVISION})\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_test_database_requires_explicit_validation_mode() -> None:\r\n    identity = {\"database_name\": \"eoat_atlas_test\", \"alembic_revision\": migration.REQUIRED_REVISION}\r\n    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match=\"test database\"):\r\n        migration.assert_safe_source(identity)\r\n    migration.assert_safe_source(identity, validation_mode=True)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_dependency_order_places_parents_before_children_and_is_stable() -> None:\r\n    meta = {\r\n        \"parent\": {\"foreign_keys\": []},\r\n        \"child_b\": {\"foreign_keys\": [{\"REFERENCED_TABLE_NAME\": \"parent\"}]},\r\n        \"child_a\": {\"foreign_keys\": [{\"REFERENCED_TABLE_NAME\": \"parent\"}]},\r\n    }\r\n    first = migration.dependency_order(meta, set(meta))\r\n    second = migration.dependency_order(meta, set(meta))\r\n    assert first == second == [\"parent\", \"child_a\", \"child_b\"]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_stable_row_order_produces_deterministic_sql() -> None:\r\n    rows = [{\"id\": 1, \"name\": \"alpha\"}, {\"id\": 2, \"name\": \"Î²eta\"}]\r\n    first = migration.insert_sql(LiteralConnection(), \"items\", rows, [\"id\", \"name\"])\r\n    second = migration.insert_sql(LiteralConnection(), \"items\", rows, [\"id\", \"name\"])\r\n    assert first == second\r\n    assert \"Î²eta\" in first[0]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_binary_and_nullable_values_are_safe() -> None:\r\n    connection = LiteralConnection()\r\n    assert migration.sql_literal(connection, b\"\\x00\\xff\") == \"X'00FF'\"\r\n    assert migration.sql_literal(connection, None) == \"NULL\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_generated_columns_are_not_part_of_insert_contract() -> None:\r\n    source = Path(migration.__file__).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n    assert '\"generated\" not in row[\"EXTRA\"].casefold()' in source\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_excluded_runtime_foreign_keys_are_cleared_and_counted() -> None:\r\n    meta = {\r\n        \"foreign_keys\": [\r\n            {\"COLUMN_NAME\": \"actor_user_id\", {\"actor_user_id\": True, \"application_instance_id\": True},\r\n    }\r\n    rows, report = migration.transform_rows(\r\n        \"entity_history_events\",\r\n        [{\"id\": 8, \"actor_user_id\": sum(report.values()) == 2\r\n\r\n\r\ndef test_required_excluded_foreign_key_is_refused() -> None:\r\n    meta = {\r\n        \"foreign_keys\": [{\"COLUMN_NAME\": \"user_id\", RuntimeError(f'{name} failed with exit code {result.returncode}')\n\ntry:\n    cnf.write_text('\\n'.join(['[client]', f\"host={values.get('EOAT_DB_HOST','127.0.0.1')}\", f\"port={values.get('EOAT_DB_PORT','3306')}\", f\"user={values.get('EOAT_DB_MIGRATION_USER', values['EOAT_DB_USER'])}\", f\"[REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT]'EOAT_DB_MIGRATION_PASSWORD', values['EOAT_DB_PASSWORD'])}\"])+'\\n', encoding='utf-8')\n    tool = [sys.executable, str(pkg / 'migration_tool.py')]\n    sql = (pkg / 'operational-data.sql').read_text(encoding='utf-8')\n    run_step('verify_package', [*tool, 'verify-package', '--package-directory', str(pkg)])\n    run_step('verify_empty_baseline', [*tool, 'verify-empty-baseline', '--package-directory', str(pkg), '--database-environment', str(env_path)])\n    run_step('sql_import', [str(mysql), f'--defaults-extra-file={cnf}', values['EOAT_DB_NAME']], input_text=sql)\n    run_step('validate_database', [*tool, 'validate-database', '--package-directory', str(pkg), '--database-environment', str(env_path)])\n    run_step('api_smoke', [*tool, 'api-smoke', '--package-directory', str(pkg), '--database-environment', str(env_path)])\n    check = subprocess.run([str(mysqlcheck), f'--defaults-extra-file={cnf}', '--check', '--extended', values['EOAT_DB_NAME']], cwd=repo, text=True, capture_output=True)\n    (pkg / 'local-mysqlcheck.txt').write_text(check.stdout + check.stderr, encoding='utf-8')\n    report['steps']['mysqlcheck_extended'] = {'returncode': check.returncode, 'status': 'PASS' if check.returncode == 0 else 'FAIL', 'stdout_tail': check.stdout[-3000:], 'stderr_tail': check.stderr[-3000:]}\n    if check.returncode: raise RuntimeError('mysqlcheck failed')\n    run_step('duplicate_import_refusal', [str(mysql), f'--defaults-extra-file={cnf}', values['EOAT_DB_NAME']], input_text=sql, expected=lambda code: code != 0)\n    run_step('post_refusal_validate_database', [*tool, 'validate-database', '--package-directory', str(pkg), '--database-environment', str(env_path)])\n    report['status'] = 'PASS'\nfinally:\n    cnf.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n    (pkg / 'local-disposable-rehearsal-evidence.json').write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding='utf-8')\nprint(json.dumps({'status': report['status'], 'steps': {name: step['status'] for name, step in report['steps'].items()}}, indent=2))\nif report['status'] != 'PASS': raise SystemExit(1)\n'@ | python const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport os\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport pymysql\nbase=Path(os.environ.get('LOCALAPPDATA',Path.home()/'AppData'/'Local'))/'EOAT Atlas Development'/'database.env';s={}\nfor raw in base.read_text(encoding='utf-8-sig').splitlines():\n line=raw.strip()\n if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n  k,v=line.split('=',1)\n  if k.strip().startswith('EOAT_'):s[k.strip()]=v.strip()\ncon=pymysql.connect(host=s.get('EOAT_DB_HOST','127.0.0.1'),port=int(s.get('EOAT_DB_PORT','3306')),user='root',password=s['EOAT_DB_ROOT_PASSWORD'])\ntry:\n with con.cursor() as c:\n  c.execute(\"SHOW DATABASES LIKE 'eoat\\\\_atlas\\\\_%'\")\n  names=[r[0] for r in c.fetchall()]\n  selected=[n for n in names if n.startswith('eoat_atlas_pkg') or n in {'eoat_atlas_validation_build_20260720t191015z','eoat_atlas_validation_build_20260720t191243z'}]\n  print({'task_owned_disposable_databases':selected,'count':len(selected)})\nfinally:con.close()\n'@ | python -","workdir":"C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction","timeout_ms":10000});

const r = await tools.shell_command({"command":"@'\nimport os,json\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport pymysql\nbase=Path(os.environ.get('LOCALAPPDATA',Path.home()/'AppData'/'Local'))/'EOAT Atlas Development'/'database.env';s={}\nfor raw in base.read_text(encoding='utf-8-sig').splitlines():\n line=raw.strip()\n if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n  k,v=line.split('=',1)\n  if k.strip().startswith('EOAT_'):s[k.strip()]=v.strip()\ncon=pymysql.connect(host=s.get('EOAT_DB_HOST','127.0.0.1'),port=int(s.get('EOAT_DB_PORT','3306')),user=s['EOAT_DB_USER'],password=s['EOAT_DB_PASSWORD'],database=s['EOAT_DB_NAME'],cursorclass=pymysql.cursors.DictCursor)\ntry:\n with con.cursor() as c:\n  c.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) value FROM eoats\");eoats=c.fetchone()['value']\n  c.execute(\"SELECT state,COUNT(*) count FROM eoat_location_observations WHERE is_authoritative=1 GROUP BY state ORDER BY state\");states=c.fetchall()\n  c.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) value FROM eoat_machine_compatibility\");machine=c.fetchone()['value']\n  c.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) value FROM eoat_tool_compatibility\");tool=c.fetchone()['value']\n  c.execute(\"SELECT ir.source_row_number, JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(ir.raw_values_json,'$.EOAT Assembly ID')) source_identifier, e.business_identifier normalized_identifier, o.state, o.machine_id, o.storage_location_id FROM import_rows ir JOIN audit_records ar ON ar.source_sheet=ir.source_sheet AND ar.source_row_number=ir.source_row_number JOIN eoats e ON e.id=ar.eoat_id JOIN eoat_location_assertions a ON a.source_row_number=ir.source_row_number JOIN eoat_location_observations o ON o.id=a.observation_id WHERE ir.source_sheet='EOAT Inventory' AND JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(ir.raw_values_json,'$.Press/Machine #'))='N/A' ORDER BY ir.source_row_number\")\n  na=c.fetchall()\n  c.execute(\"SELECT ir.source_row_number,JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(ir.raw_values_json,'$.EOAT Assembly ID')) identifier,JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(ir.raw_values_json,'$.Press/Machine #')) raw_machine,m.machine_number normalized_machine FROM import_rows ir JOIN audit_records ar ON ar.source_sheet=ir.source_sheet AND ar.source_row_number=ir.source_row_number JOIN machines m ON m.id=ar.machine_id WHERE ir.source_sheet='EOAT Inventory' AND JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(ir.raw_values_json,'$.Press/Machine #'))='26 - Xqual in 25'\")\n  xqual=c.fetchall()\n  c.execute(\"SELECT JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(resolution_notes,'$.status')) status,COUNT(*) count FROM import_issues GROUP BY status ORDER BY status\")\n  issues=c.fetchall()\n print(json.dumps({'eoats':eoats,'state_totals':states,'machine_compatibility':machine,'tool_compatibility':tool,'n_a_rows':na,'xqual_corrections':xqual,'import_issue_resolutions':issues},indent=2))\nfinally:con.close()\n'@ | python -","workdir":"C:\\Users\\kgray\\eoat-atlas-production-location-correction","timeout_ms":30000});

Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.\\n\\nSave a site version only after validating and pushing its source. commit_sha must be the current HEAD of the site's configured source branch. Any archive must come from that exact source state and contain a deployable Sites build. Saving does not deploy the version. Retain version_id for follow-up calls and report the user-facing version number. This tool is part of plugin `Sites`.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { mcp__codex_apps__sites_save_site_version(args: {\\n  // Optional site build tar archive from the source identified by commit_sha. It must contain a supported OpenNext or vinext entrypoint and a valid .openai/hosting.json. This parameter expects an absolute local file path. If you want to upload a file, provide the absolute path to that file here.\\n  archive?: string;\\n  // Git commit SHA for the current HEAD of the site's configured source branch. It must identify the source used to build the archive.\\n  commit_sha: string;\\n  // Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.\\n  project_id: string;\\n}): Promise<CallToolResult<{ result: {\\n  archive_storage?: { archive_format: string; content_hash: string; file_count?: number | null; sediment_file_id: string; size_bytes?: number | null; } | null;\\n  // Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.\\n  id: string;\\n  // Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.\\n  project_id: string;\\n  screenshot_url?: string | null;\\n  source: { commit_sha: string; };\\n  version_number: number;\\n}; }>>; };\\n```\"},{\"name\":\"mcp__codex_apps__sites_update_environment_variables\",\"description\":\"Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.\\n\\nUpdate production runtime environment variables for a site. Only listed keys change; all others remain unchanged. Store runtime values in Sites, not .openai/hosting.json. Deploy a saved version after any change to apply the new environment revision. This tool is part of plugin `Sites`.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { mcp__codex_apps__sites_update_environment_variables(args: {\\n  // Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.\\n  project_id: string;\\n  // Case-sensitive environment keys to remove. Do not repeat keys or include a key also present in set_values. Omit or pass an empty list to preserve other keys.\\n  remove?: Array<string> | null;\\n  // Environment entries to create or replace. Keys are case-sensitive and must match the application. Do not repeat keys or include a key also listed in remove. Mark sensitive values as secrets.\\n  set_values: Array<{\\n  // Set true for sensitive values so they are not returned in plaintext.\\n  is_secret?: boolean;\\n  // Required non-empty, case-sensitive environment variable name.\\n  key: string;\\n  type?: \\\"envvar\\\";\\n  value: string;\\n}>;\\n}): Promise<CallToolResult<{ result: { entries: Array<{ is_secret?: boolean; key: string; type?: \\\"envvar\\\"; value: string | null; }>; project_id: string; revision: number; updated_at: string | null; }; }>>; };\\n```\"},{\"name\":\"mcp__codex_apps__sites_update_site_access\",\"description\":\"Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Always use Sites when .openai/hosting.json exists. Use Sites skills for local implementation, validation, source preparation, and artifact packaging. Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.\\n\\nUpdate who can visit a site only when the user asks to change access. The owner always remains allowed. For workspace sites, call list_available_access_groups before adding groups and use only the IDs the user selects. This tool is part of plugin `Sites`.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { mcp__codex_apps__sites_update_site_access(args: {\\n  // Required access mode for the site: public grants anyone with the URL; workspace_all grants all active workspace users; custom uses the supplied user and group allowlists.\\n  access_mode: \\\"public\\\" | \\\"workspace_all\\\" | \\\"custom\\\";\\n  // Tenant group ID allowlist. IDs must come from list_available_access_groups and belong to the tenant linked to the site workspace. Omit to preserve the existing allowlist; pass an empty list to clear it.\\n  allowed_tenant_group_ids?: Array<string> | null;\\n  // User email allowlist. Emails must belong to active users in the site workspace. Omit to preserve the existing allowlist; pass an empty list to clear non-owner users.\\n  allowed_user_emails?: Array<string> | null;\\n  // Workspace group ID allowlist. IDs must come from list_available_access_groups and belong to the site workspace. Omit to preserve the existing allowlist; pass an empty list to clear it.\\n  allowed_workspace_group_ids?: Array<string> | null;\\n  // Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.\\n  project_id: string;\\n}): Promise<CallToolResult<{ result: {\\n  // Access mode for the app.\\n  access_mode: \\\"public\\\" | \\\"admins_only\\\" | \\\"workspace_all\\\" | \\\"custom\\\";\\n  // Account user ID allowlist for the app.\\n  allowed_account_user_ids: Array<string>;\\n  // Group details resolved from allowed workspace and tenant group IDs.\\n  allowed_groups: Array<{\\n  // Group ID to use in an Appgen access policy.\\n  id: string;\\n  // Group display name.\\n  name: string;\\n  // Total number of members in the group.\\n  size: number;\\n}>;\\n  // Tenant group ID allowlist for the app.\\n  allowed_tenant_group_ids: Array<string>;\\n  // User details resolved from allowed_account_user_ids.\\n  allowed_users: Array<{\\n  // Account user ID for the allowed user.\\n  account_user_id: string;\\n  // Email address for the allowed user, when available.\\n  email?: string | null;\\n  // Display name for the allowed user, when available.\\n  name?: string | null;\\n}>;\\n  // Workspace group ID allowlist for the app.\\n  allowed_workspace_group_ids: Array<string>;\\n  // Appgen project ID\\n  project_id: string;\\n  // Monotonic access policy revision.\\n  revision: number;\\n  // Access policy update timestamp.\\n  updated_at: string;\\n}; }>>; };\\n```\"},{\"name\":\"mcp__codex_apps__sites_update_site_metadata\",\"description\":\"Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Always use Sites when .openai/hosting.json exists. Use Sites skills for local implementation, validation, source preparation, and artifact packaging. Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.\\n\\nUpdate a site's metadata. Currently, this supports changing its display title; it does not change the site's URL or slug. This tool is part of plugin `Sites`.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { mcp__codex_apps__sites_update_site_metadata(args: {\\n  // Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.\\n  project_id: string;\\n  // New user-facing site title.\\n  title: string;\\n}): Promise<CallToolResult<{ result: {\\n  auth_client_id: string | null;\\n  created_at: string;\\n  current_live_url: string | null;\\n  current_preview_url: string | null;\\n  description: string | null;\\n  disabled_by?: \\\"workspace_admin\\\" | \\\"openai\\\" | null;\\n  // Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.\\n  id: string;\\n  latest_version_number: number;\\n  screenshot_url: string | null;\\n  slug: string;\\n  status: \\\"active\\\" | \\\"archived\\\" | \\\"suspended\\\";\\n  title: string;\\n  updated_at: string;\\n}; }>>; };\\n```\"},{\"name\":\"shell_command\",\"description\":\"Runs a Powershell command (Windows) and returns its output.\\n\\nExamples of valid command strings:\\n\\n- ls -a (show hidden): \\\"Get-ChildItem -Force\\\"\\n- recursive find by name: \\\"Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.py\\\"\\n- recursive grep: \\\"Get-ChildItem -Path C:\\\\\\\\myrepo -Recurse | Select-String -Pattern 'TODO' -CaseSensitive\\\"\\n- ps aux | grep python: \\\"Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -like '*python*' }\\\"\\n- setting an env var: \\\"$env:FOO='bar'; echo $env:FOO\\\"\\n- running an inline Python script: \\\"@'\\\\\\\\nprint('Hello, world!')\\\\\\\\n'@ | python -\\\"\\n\\nWindows safety rules:\\n- Do not compose destructive filesystem commands across shells. Do not enumerate paths in PowerShell and then pass them to `cmd /c`, batch builtins, or another shell for deletion or moving. Use one shell end-to-end, prefer native PowerShell cmdlets such as `Remove-Item` / `Move-Item` with `-LiteralPath`, and avoid string-built shell commands for file operations.\\n- Before any recursive delete or move on Windows, verify the resolved absolute target paths stay within the intended workspace or explicitly named target directory. Never issue a recursive delete or move against a computed path if the final target has not been checked.\\n- When using `Start-Process` to launch a background helper or service, pass `-WindowStyle Hidden` unless the user explicitly asked for a visible interactive window. Use visible windows only for interactive tools the user needs to see or control.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { shell_command(args: {\\n  // Shell script to run in the user's default shell.\\n  command: string;\\n  // User-facing approval question for `require_escalated`; omit otherwise.\\n  justification?: string;\\n  // True runs with login shell semantics; false disables them. Defaults to true.\\n  login?: boolean;\\n  // Reusable approval prefix for `cmd`, only with `sandbox_permissions: \\\"require_escalated\\\"`; for example [\\\"git\\\", \\\"pull\\\"].\\n  prefix_rule?: Array<string>;\\n  // Per-command sandbox override. Defaults to `use_default`; use `require_escalated` for unsandboxed execution.\\n  sandbox_permissions?: \\\"use_default\\\" | \\\"require_escalated\\\";\\n  // Maximum command runtime. Defaults to 10000 ms.\\n  timeout_ms?: number;\\n  // Working directory for the command. Defaults to the turn cwd.\\n  workdir?: string;\\n}): Promise<unknown>; };\\n```\"}]", "type": "input_text"}]

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T20:13:09.830Z

const results = ALL_TOOLS
  .filter(x => /thread.*terminal|terminal.*(write|send|input|command)|interactive.*terminal/i.test(`${x.name} ${x.description}`))
  .map(x => ({ name: x.name, description: x.description.slice(0, 220) }));
text(JSON.stringify(results));

**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T20:14:15.108Z

The standalone SSH shell cannot be reused by automated deployment commands, and password authentication does not create a reusable credential. I’m opening a second interactive session configured as a local SSH control master; after you enter the password there, deployment commands can securely reuse that authenticated connection without handling the password.

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n# Packaging EOAT Atlas\r\n\r\nEOAT Atlas is distributed as a Windows PyInstaller `onedir` application, a lightweight launcher, and a no-elevation\r\nper-user installer bundle. Packaging is an internal qualification workflow; it does not imply production approval or\r\ncode signing.\r\n\r\n## Prerequisites\r\n\r\n- Windows 10 or newer.\r\n- Python 3.12.\r\n- A clean committed checkout.\r\n- Runtime dependencies installed from `requirements.lock` and build dependencies from `requirements-build.lock`.\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npython -m pip install -r requirements-build.lock\r\npython -m PyInstaller --version\r\n```\r\n\r\nPyInstaller is build tooling and is not included in the runtime lock. UPX is disabled for both application and launcher\r\nbuilds. No signing identity is configured; an approved certificate and IT process are external requirements.\r\n\r\n## Build and verify the application\r\n\r\nFrom the repository root:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npython scripts/build_package.py\r\npython scripts/smoke_test_package.py \"dist/EOAT Atlas/EOAT Atlas.exe\"\r\n```\r\n\r\n`build_package.py` refuses a dirty tree unless the explicit development-only\r\n`EOAT_ATLAS_ALLOW_DIRTY_BUILD=1` override is set. A production-candidate build must not use that override. The generated\r\npackage metadata records the exact commit, branch, timestamp, build run, dirty state, build ID, and executable SHA-256.\r\nThe package manifest records every packaged file and checksum.\r\n\r\n## Build the Debian server release\r\n\r\nCommit the intended source first, then build its exact revision:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npython scripts/release/build_server_release.py --source-commit HEAD --branch-name fix/release-provenance\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe builder refuses relevant dirty server files, reads the version and defaults from the selected commit, creates a\r\nnormalized ZIP under `dist/server`, inserts generated `release_metadata.json`, and writes adjacent SHA-256 and JSON\r\nmanifest files. Reusing the same explicit `--build-timestamp` with the same commit produces the same metadata, build ID,\r\nZIP ordering, timestamps, permissions, and checksum. The manifestâ€”not the tracked treeâ€”binds the source commit to the\r\nfinished archive checksum. The archive intentionally contains no `.git` directory and needs none at runtime.\r\n\r\nWritable runtime state belongs under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\EOAT_Atlas`; it must not be written into the onedir package.\r\n\r\n## Build the launcher\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npython scripts/build_launcher.py\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe launcher is written to `dist\\launcher\\EOAT Atlas Launcher.exe`. It resolves the current per-user application,\r\nvalidates release/update identity, prevents duplicate launch, and records diagnostics under the per-user runtime root.\r\n\r\n## Build the per-user installer bundle\r\n\r\nBuild the application and launcher first, then run:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npowershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -File installer/Build_Installer_Exe.ps1 -PythonExe python -Clean\r\n```\r\n\r\n`installer\\dist` is the distributable folder. It contains only the no-elevation installer entry point, its audited\r\nruntime script/configuration, and the application and launcher payloads. Installation is per-user and refuses elevated\r\nexecution. Validate an installed instance with `installer/Validate_EOAT_Atlas_Install.ps1`.\r\n\r\nThe configured desktop shortcut is intentional and targets the launcher. Application binaries are versioned under the\r\nper-user application root; logs, cache, and user identity follow the retention behavior documented in\r\n`installer/README_INSTALLER.md`.\r\n\r\n## Exclusions and security\r\n\r\nPackages must not contain `.env` files, credentials, Git metadata, private workbooks, operational evidence, test\r\nfixtures, developer caches, or backup artifacts. Endpoint allowlisting, certificate-backed signing, production TLS,\r\nproduction infrastructure, and deployment approval remain external IT controls.\r\nusage: build_server_release.py [-h] [--source-commit SOURCE_COMMIT]\r\n                               [--branch-name BRANCH_NAME]\r\n                               [--build-timestamp BUILD_TIMESTAMP]\r\n                               [--output-dir OUTPUT_DIR]\r\n\r\nBuild a deterministic EOAT Atlas server release from one Git commit\r\n\r\noptions:\r\n  -h, --help            show this help message and exit\r\n  --source-commit SOURCE_COMMIT\r\n                        Exact commit-ish to resolve and package\r\n  --branch-name BRANCH_NAME\r\n                        Branch recorded in generated metadata\r\n  --build-timestamp BUILD_TIMESTAMP\r\n                        UTC timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ); defaults to\r\n                        current UTC\r\n  --output-dir OUTPUT_DIR\r\n{\r\n  \"app_name\": \"EOAT Atlas\",\r\n  \"api_contract_version\": \"1.4.0\",\r\n  \"database_schema_revision\": \"20260717_0007\",\r\n  \"environment\": \"production\",\r\n  \"release_channel\": \"server\",\r\n [Task 2]\\n247:- The installer stages `App\\\\.staging`, validates metadata/file count/bytes, finalizes under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\\\EOAT_Atlas\\\\App\\\\<release_id>`, preserves runtime identity/data, archives prior versions, and uses `robocopy.exe /E /COPY:DAT /DCOPY:DAT /R:2 /W:1 /XJ` for deep package paths. Canonical deployment is under `01_App\\\\Releases`, `01_App\\\\Installer`, and `01_App\\\\Launcher`; run `Install_EOAT_Atlas.ps1 -ValidateOnly`.
